title: Wuphf.Com

jokes.text: Uh-oh. Okay, okay, nobody panic. Listen up, listen up. Everyone, follow me to the shelter. We've got enough food for 14 days. After that, we have a difficult conversation.
jokes.setup: Something minor has gone wrong (implied power outage)
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately escalates to apocalypse survival mode with ominous implications about post-food discussions
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight overreaction perfectly integrated with his survivalist character. The 'difficult conversation' euphemism is darkly funny.

jokes.text: Try zero, zero, zero, zero, zero, one.
jokes.setup: After trying basic passwords like 'password' and all zeros
jokes.punchline: The logical next step is somehow changing just one digit
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about inefficient password guessing methods

jokes.text: Okay, I'm not doing every number.
jokes.setup: Being asked to try sequential password combinations
jokes.punchline: Stating the obvious futility with exasperation
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction to absurd request

jokes.text: Our IT guys have been... Glasses, Turban, Ear Hair, Fatty 3, Shorts, Fatty 2, Lozenge, and Fatso.
jokes.setup: Trying to remember IT staff names professionally
jokes.punchline: Michael's completely inappropriate physical descriptors instead of names, with detailed categorization system
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael Scott insensitivity. The systematic naming convention ('Fatty 2' vs 'Fatty 3') makes it funnier and more cringey.

jokes.text: You're typing 'big boobs'? / Try 'big boobz' with a Z.
jokes.setup: Someone typing inappropriate password guesses
jokes.punchline: Making it even more juvenile by suggesting the 'z' variation
jokes.explanation: Juvenile humor but the escalation from bad to worse password suggestions has comedic structure

jokes.text: The password. We're in. / This kept us secure, people.
jokes.setup: 'Big boobz' actually works as the password
jokes.punchline: Michael spinning this juvenile password as good security
jokes.explanation: Great ironic spin from Michael. The absurdity that this was actually the password plus his defensive rationalization works well.

jokes.text: At some point, somebody is going to have to step up and do my taxes, and it is not going to be me.
jokes.setup: Michael discussing responsibilities
jokes.punchline: Declaring he won't do his own taxes as if it's someone else's job
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael Scott entitlement and avoidance of responsibility

jokes.text: Get out. Do your job. / What-- / Yes.
jokes.setup: Michael invites someone to talk, then immediately
jokes.punchline: Contradicts himself by dismissing them brusquely
jokes.explanation: Michael's classic inconsistency and poor management style

jokes.text: Looks like your little documentary finally found its star.
jokes.setup: The documentary crew filming everyone
jokes.punchline: Ryan's narcissistic assumption he's now the main character
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor that plays with the documentary format while showcasing Ryan's ego

jokes.text: Yes, I invested in WUPHF. It's actually a really great idea, and I can't believe it didn't exist before. And I know Ryan's kind of a sketchy guy, but, hey, I married Jim. I've done my part for the nice guy. Now I want a bedroom set--One that matches.
jokes.setup: Pam explaining her investment decision
jokes.punchline: Reducing her marriage to Jim as 'doing her part for nice guys' and now wanting material gain
jokes.explanation: Subversive take on Pam's character - cynical pragmatism dressed as romantic realism. The specific 'bedroom set that matches' detail is perfectly mundane.

jokes.text: Every fall, growing up, my uncle Eldred used to build us a maze out of hay bales for us kids to play in. We called it Hay Place. Eldred called it Hay World. Eventually lawyers were brought in. But that's all behind us. Hay Place lives on.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his hay maze inspiration
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of legal action over a children's hay maze naming dispute
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Dwight backstory. The escalation from innocent family fun to legal action is beautifully understated, and 'Hay Place lives on' is triumphant and ridiculous.

jokes.text: Do you like dogs? / I have, like, eight dogs. / Hay Place romances never last.
jokes.setup: Dwight hitting on someone at his hay festival
jokes.punchline: His immediate cynical dismissal of the potential romance he just initiated
jokes.explanation: Excellent character beat - Dwight's immediate pessimism undercuts his own romantic attempt. Great economy in the delivery.

jokes.text: I am on the first hot sale streak of my life. I think it all comes from feeding Cece, because no matter how much she resists, I sell her those carrots. Let's be honest--If I can make mushed carrots seem better than a boob... I can pretty much sell anything.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining his sales success
jokes.punchline: Comparing selling food to a baby to his professional sales skills, with crude but honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Smart observational humor about parenthood. The boob comparison is crude but genuinely insightful about baby feeding challenges.

jokes.text: I entered the sale, and I hit enter, and I said, 'dunh-duh-na-dah!' I'm pretty sure I timesed it right.
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining his commission calculation
jokes.punchline: His made-up math terminology and sound effects for entering data
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin dialogue - the sound effects and 'timesed' are quintessential Kevin malapropisms

jokes.text: Dunh-duh-nah-dah! Still zero.
jokes.setup: Kevin repeating his calculation with the same sound effect
jokes.punchline: Same disappointing result despite the fanfare
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces the futility while staying in character

jokes.text: I wish my gym didn't allow full nudity in the locker room. Okay, some of these old guys walking around naked--It feels almost passive-aggressive.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to make an analogy about unwanted policies
jokes.punchline: His specific complaint about gym nudity being 'passive-aggressive'
jokes.explanation: Michael's discomfort with nudity and interpretation of it as intentional aggression is character-appropriate

jokes.text: Think about your commission cap as a naked old man in a gym locker room.
jokes.setup: Michael's failed analogy about accepting unwanted policies
jokes.punchline: The completely unhelpful and nonsensical final comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of Michael's terrible analogy. The absurd comparison makes no sense but feels like Michael logic.

jokes.text: Excuse me, everyone. Sex! Now that I have your attention... You don't have our attention. / Money! I'm listening.
jokes.setup: Michael's failed attempt to get attention with 'Sex'
jokes.punchline: Stanley only responding to 'Money'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Stanley's single-minded focus on money over Michael's attention-seeking

jokes.text: Yes, I have a dream. And it's not some MLK dream for equality. I want to own a decommissioned lighthouse. And I want to live at the top. And nobody knows I live there. And there's a button that I can press and launch that lighthouse into space.
jokes.setup: Creed referencing MLK's famous dream speech
jokes.punchline: His completely bizarre and specific antisocial lighthouse fantasy
jokes.explanation: Peak Creed absurdism. The MLK reference makes it inappropriately funny, and the lighthouse space-launch fantasy is wonderfully specific and insane. This is near-legendary Creed content.

jokes.text: We already have a buyer. / Really? Who? / Washington University.
jokes.setup: Impressive buyer for the company
jokes.punchline: Revealed later to only want it for the domain name acronym
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the later reveal about the domain name. Works better in context of the full episode.

jokes.text: Bob's finances are... Complicated. He only invests with cash and only in nightclubs.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Bob won't invest
jokes.punchline: The suspicious specificity of his investment preferences
jokes.explanation: Subtle implication about Bob Vance's potentially shady business dealings

jokes.text: You could buy the Bernard Estate and just burn it to the ground while your entire family watched. / That is horrible. I love my family.
jokes.setup: Michael assuming Andy has family resentment
jokes.punchline: Andy's genuine affection for his family deflating Michael's pitch
jokes.explanation: Michael's projection fails when Andy actually has a healthy family relationship

jokes.text: That's a safety school, Michael. / And they laughed you off of campus, right? So who's laughing now?
jokes.setup: Andy dismissing Washington University
jokes.punchline: Michael turning Andy's Cornell rejection into motivation
jokes.explanation: Plays on Andy's educational insecurities and Michael's manipulative sales tactics

jokes.text: Actually, I'm kind of in the mood for a roll in the hay. / Roll in the hay, $5, please.
jokes.setup: Angela using sexual innuendo
jokes.punchline: Dwight literally charging for the hay activity
jokes.explanation: Good play on the double meaning, with Dwight's money-focused literalism

jokes.text: No, no. Oscar, it's not--it's not a digital rape whistle.
jokes.setup: Oscar suggesting emergency notification use
jokes.punchline: Ryan's terrible comparison rejecting the idea
jokes.explanation: Appropriately uncomfortable Ryan moment but relies on shock value over wit

jokes.text: I don't feel like skiing right now, Oscar.
jokes.setup: Oscar pointing out Michael can ski without the business trip
jokes.punchline: Michael's petulant response treating skiing as mood-dependent
jokes.explanation: Good Michael childishness - treating recreational skiing like it requires specific business circumstances

jokes.text: Well, since I have no incentive to sell, I've cleaned my car, organized my desk, and taken a multivitamin. So... What now?
jokes.setup: Jim having no motivation due to commission cap
jokes.punchline: His productive but non-work activities and casual request for direction
jokes.explanation: Jim's passive-aggressive response to corporate policy through productive procrastination

jokes.text: I found the needle in the haystack! / Hey, congratulations. Do you know what your prize is? Nothing. Life lesson? Some tasks are not worth doing.
jokes.setup: Someone excited about finding the needle
jokes.punchline: Dwight's nihilistic lesson about effort and reward
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight philosophy - taking a fun activity and making it existentially bleak

jokes.text: WUPHF condoms! 50,000 condoms out of the sky. / Look at that! He threw 'em on the ground!
jokes.setup: Ryan's elaborate Spring Break marketing fantasy
jokes.punchline: The visual of Ryan physically throwing props during his pitch
jokes.explanation: Ryan's delusional marketing fantasy with physical comedy element

jokes.text: My aunt and uncle have a house in the Poconos. I can borrow it... Just go up there, shut myself away-- No TV, no WUPHF-- Just, like, play a video game.
jokes.setup: Ryan explaining his business planning retreat
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting 'no TV' with playing video games
jokes.explanation: Ryan's self-deception about productivity and focus, immediately undermined

jokes.text: I said to Ryan, 'I try to call you, and you don't have your phone. I try to IM you, and you're not online. I wish there was a way that I could do everything all at once, and I could just be like this little dog going, Ruff! Ruff! Ruff! Ruff! Ruff!' And that's how it happened, and that's what I told my lawyer.
jokes.setup: Kelly claiming she invented WUPHF
jokes.punchline: Her ridiculous dog barking origin story and mention of involving lawyers
jokes.explanation: Peak Kelly - the absurd dog metaphor, immediate escalation to legal action, and complete self-centeredness are perfectly in character

jokes.text: They only want it for the initials. The domain name. / Oh, God. / W.U.P.H.F.
jokes.setup: The Washington University buyer reveal
jokes.punchline: Realization that WUPHF spells out the university's public health fund
jokes.explanation: Clever reveal that deflates the 'buyer' excitement - they just want the domain coincidence

jokes.text: Yeah, so we can celebrate Thanksgiving-me-your-money day. / That's humorous. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Jim's pun on Thanksgiving
jokes.punchline: Overly formal acknowledgment of the humor
jokes.explanation: Weak pun made awkward by the formal response - more cringe than clever

jokes.text: Eight years. / Listen, I know you really like Ryan-- / Well, why have I been separating the trash into whites and colors?
jokes.setup: Serious conversation about recycling and Ryan
jokes.punchline: Michael's complete misunderstanding of waste sorting
jokes.explanation: Michael treating recycling like laundry sorting shows his fundamental misunderstanding of basic concepts

jokes.text: You may be crazy. / Don't. / I'm just saying that I think I agree with your point. / Yeah, but in a jokey way where you're gonna start singing.
jokes.setup: Michael starting to reference Billy Joel song lyrics
jokes.punchline: Jim recognizing and stopping Michael's musical tendency
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about Michael's tendency to break into song during serious moments

jokes.text: The moment Darla put the cupcake in her mouth, her daddy pulled her aside and said, 'You're too fat. No one's gonna like you if you're too fat.'
jokes.setup: Gabe reading from what appears to be his novel
jokes.punchline: Disturbing childhood trauma content delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Appropriately uncomfortable Gabe content - disturbing but fits his awkward character

jokes.text: You and me on a private plane flown by our private pilot... / Yeah, well, we never even have dinner now, so...
jokes.setup: Ryan painting grand success fantasy
jokes.punchline: Michael's reality check about their current non-relationship
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Ryan's fantasy with simple truth about their actual relationship

jokes.text: We appear to have a three-way tie. I have no choice but to pick the Hay King myself. I pick... Me. I am your Hay King!
jokes.setup: Democratic process to choose Hay King
jokes.punchline: Dwight rigging the competition to crown himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of Dwight's childhood trauma - rigging his own competition to achieve what he always wanted

jokes.text: Did I truck 300 bales of hay to a parking lot to rectify some childhood disappointment? Yes.
jokes.setup: Talking head reflecting on the hay festival
jokes.punchline: Completely honest assessment of his psychological motivations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware moment. The clinical tone combined with the absurd but understandable motivation makes this very quotable.

jokes.text: Why would I let someone use me? That doesn't make any sense. / What makes you think someone's using you? / Ryan had an idea for a business. / Ryan-- Uh, yeah, okay.
jokes.setup: Michael denying he's being used
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealing Ryan's manipulation in his very next words
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment where Michael immediately contradicts his denial

jokes.text: Like, if you took a person, turned him into a car, and then used the car and then somebody bought the car and started racing with the car, started beating everybody else on the track and this car was, like, human--
jokes.setup: Michael trying to explain feeling used through metaphor
jokes.punchline: Completely convoluted car-person transformation analogy
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael over-complicated analogy that makes no sense but shows his thought process

jokes.text: Well, maybe you should handle your own situation now that you know that Jason is banging your wife. / My wife passed away a few years ago.
jokes.setup: Michael making crude assumption about therapist's marriage
jokes.punchline: Devastating revelation that the wife is deceased
jokes.explanation: Brutal comedic timing that makes Michael's inappropriate comment backfire spectacularly

jokes.text: Consider it a WUPHF in person.
jokes.setup: Ryan confronting people who want to sell
jokes.punchline: Applying his business concept to real-world harassment
jokes.explanation: Ryan trying to make his obnoxious behavior sound innovative

jokes.text: I would rather go broke betting on my people than get rich all by myself on some island like a castaway. And there is no middle ground.
jokes.setup: Michael defending his investment in Ryan
jokes.punchline: Declaring false binary choice with dramatic island metaphor
jokes.explanation: Michael's misguided loyalty expressed through dramatic but nonsensical metaphor

jokes.text: Mother Earth by Dwight Kurt Schrute. The water runs, the sun will glint. This is our environment. The Hay King steps upon the ground. He wields a giant man sword round. He penetrates the Mother Earth, once, twice, again and again and again and again for all he's worth, pounding into her soft, warm dirt. Her lips quivering, mounds shaking in ecstasy. And sudden relief.
jokes.setup: Dwight reading what appears to be environmental poetry
jokes.punchline: Increasingly sexual metaphors disguised as nature poetry
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight weirdness - genuinely poetic language that becomes increasingly inappropriate. The escalation and his serious delivery make it funnier.

jokes.text: The world sends people your way. Ryan came to me through a temp agency. Andy was transferred here. No idea where Creed came from. The point is, you just have to play with the cards that you're dealt. Jim, that guy is an ace. Dwight is my king up my sleeve. Phyllis is my old maid. Oscar is my queen. That's easy. Give me a hard one. That's what Oscar said. Toby is the instruction card you throw away. Pam's a solid seven. And yeah, you know what? Ryan is probably, like, a two. But sometimes twos can be wild. So watch out. And I am obviously the joker.
jokes.setup: Michael's card game metaphor for his employees
jokes.punchline: The entire extended metaphor with specific card assignments, including self-deprecating joker designation
jokes.explanation: Great extended metaphor that reveals Michael's view of everyone. The 'That's what Oscar said' insertion and calling himself the joker show self-awareness.

jokes.text: Gabe Lewis. Now, listen here, Gabe, you're too fat. No one's gonna like you if you're too fat.
jokes.setup: Gabe continuing his disturbing novel reading
jokes.punchline: Applying the earlier trauma dialogue to Gabe himself
jokes.explanation: Callback that makes Gabe's writing even more uncomfortably personal

tmdb_overview: Michael tries to save Ryan's company by encouraging co-workers to invest. Meanwhile, Dwight has big plans for the office at Thanksgiving.
